He held himself over her, not yet withdrawing. After crossing into the White, her warmth and softness was comforting, a tether back to reality as much as it was a reminder of their shared pleasure. There was no tension evident in her, no fear. No disgust for having coupled with a bot again. For a few moments, at the very least, he’d made her happy . Something about that was…fulfilling. Ronin’s brow furrowed. Why should his contentment be derived from her enjoyment? He was a dustwalker, a wanderer, a robot who would walk the wastes until he was disassembled or deactivated. And yet, after so many years of searching, it was only here with this woman that he was closest to discovering his purpose . His thoughts were interrupted by a long, low gurgling. Lara peeked at him from behind her arm . “What was that?” he asked . “My stomach .” He leaned back and moved his hands to her abdomen, checking for more bruises, feeling for sign of damage. She squirmed, body shaking. He was causing her more pain . “What are you doing?” She took hold of his wrists, pushing them away. Her smile hadn’t faltered . “I hurt you,” he replied, tilting his head . She shook again, and this time, laughter bubbled from her. “No, you didn’t .” The both looked at her abdomen when the sound came again . “How could I not have ?” “That means I’m hungry , Ronin .” Tentatively, he pressed his palm to her stomach. She didn’t stop him. He could feel the rumbling inside. Whatever data his fractured memory possessed on human anatomy, it apparently didn’t cover their bodily functions with much depth . “You eat, and this stops ?” “ Yup .” “So…there’s no pain ?” She looked away . “ Well …” He withdrew his hand immediately . “ Lara …” She chuckled. “Pain in all the right places .” “I don—” He snapped his mouth shut as he recalled Lara digging her nails into his skin, pulling his hair, pressing her heels into his thighs. Each a small source of pain that had heightened the experience. “I think I understand. Somewhat.” His optics dropped to where their bodies were still connected. He lowered a hand to her hip, settling his fingers over the bruises. “Pain isn’t always bad…because pain is part of being alive .” “Yeah, as long as it’s not too much.” Her smile wavered, and something flashed in her eyes for an instant. Before he could guess its nature, she recovered. “I’m fine, Ronin. Just starving. Not, like, for real.” She pulled away from him and sat up. He immediately craved her heat. “Lemme get something to eat, and you can show me the scrap you found while you were gone .” “All right.” He watched Lara slip out of bed, noting how her figure had filled out subtly since she started living with him. He knew she wasn’t fine, not under the surface, and how could she be? Not all pain was physical. It had only been nine and a half hours since he told her about Tabitha . She plucked a shirt off the floor and tugged it on, glancing over her shoulder to cast another smile at him. Reaching up to tuck some of her loose hair behind her ear, she walked into the hallway, leaving his vision when she turned onto the stairs . Ronin climbed out of bed, pulled on his pants, and picked up his coat. As he walked, he dipped a hand into the inside coat pocket, withdrawing the ring . Perhaps it would be an uplifting surprise for her, on the heels of so strong a loss . Chapter Eighteen L ara looked over the items spread on the worktable. Tools, scraps of metal and plastic, spare bot parts, and a few power cells. It was more than she’d found over years of scavenging.
